patient_profile:
Here is a brief patient profile:

Meet Sarah, a 28-year-old marketing specialist who has been feeling overwhelmed with work demands lately. She's been consistently working long hours, often sacrificing her free time to meet deadlines and keep up with an increasingly competitive industry. Despite her best efforts, she's struggled to get quality sleep in the past few weeks, finding herself lying awake at night thinking about tasks that need attention. Her social life has also suffered, with friends commenting on how infrequent her appearances have become.

This patient profile describes Sarah's daily struggles, work-life balance, and recent changes that may be contributing to her minimal mental health concerns.

conversation.content: Hi Sarah, thanks for coming in today. How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I've just been really overwhelmed with work. It's like my to-do list is always growing and I'm running out of hours in the day.
I see. What are some things that have been on your mind lately?
Just stuff at work, you know? Trying to keep up with all these projects and meeting deadlines. It's exhausting.
Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine that might be contributing to this feeling of exhaustion?
Yeah, I've been having a tough time winding down at night too. I'll just lie there thinking about all the things I need to do tomorrow.
That sounds really tough. Have you had any difficulty sleeping because of this?
Not really... not exactly, anyway. I mean, I'll go to bed at a reasonable hour, but then I just can't turn my brain off.
I see. And what about your appetite? Has that changed at all?
Hmm, actually... yeah. I've been having a harder time finding the motivation to cook or try new recipes lately.
That's interesting. Have you noticed any changes in your social life too?
Yeah... it feels like my friends are getting tired of me canceling plans or just not being as available as I used to be.
I'm so sorry to hear that. It sounds like you're feeling pretty overwhelmed and isolated right now.
Yeah, it feels like nobody understands what I'm going through or how to help me.
Sarah, can you tell me a little bit about what you're hoping to get out of our sessions today?
I guess... I just want to feel like myself again. Like I'm not drowning in all this work stuff.
That makes a lot of sense, Sarah. And we're definitely going to work on that together.
